analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: Every Q/K/V/output projection GEMM (4096x512 @ 512x512) runs on the FP32 CUDA-core SIMT path, not the Tensor Cores. cuBLAS dispatches FP32 mm to the generic SIMT GEMM ('Kernel2'): smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0 and tensor_core_active_pct = 0.0 across all 9 mm kernels, while smsp__average_thread_inst_executed_per_inst_executed.ratio = 32.0 confirms a fully-scalar 32-thread/warp FMA loop. sm__throughput is stuck at ~36% with achieved_occupancy ~16.5% (capped by registers_per_thread = 210). The CUTLASS attention kernel is the FP32 variant (fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80): it does use Tensor Cores (58.7%) but spills heavily (l1tex__t_output_wavefronts_pipe_lsu_mem_local.sum = 757760 local-memory wavefronts, registers_per_thread = 168, occupancy 14%) because the FP32 register footprint exceeds the file. The 5th-gen Blackwell Tensor Cores are almost entirely idle on the dominant compute.

transformation.description: Insert dtype casts so all attention-block GEMMs and the SDPA inputs execute in BF16 (or at minimum TF32). Casting the mm operands to bfloat16 reroutes cuBLAS from the SIMT 'Kernel2' path to the HMMA Tensor-Core path, and rerouting Q/K/V to bf16 makes SDPA select the bf16 CUTLASS/flash fmha kernel, eliminating the FP32 register-spill (757760 local-memory wavefronts -> ~0). GPU-level effect: HMMA pipe replaces scalar FMA loop (tensor_core_active 0% -> engaged), register pressure drops (210 -> well under the limit) so occupancy and SM throughput rise, and the FP32 fmha spill path is removed. Apply at the Aten IR level by inserting aten.convert_element_type before each mm/SDPA consumer and casting the layernorm output back as needed; weights are cast once and re-registered as bf16 buffers.
transformation.location: FX backend pass run on the Inductor pre-grad / Aten graph, before lowering
fx_steps: node.replace_all_uses_with(back, delete_user_cb=lambda u: u is not back)
q, k, v = node.args[0], node.args[1], node.args[2]
node.update_arg(0, qb); node.update_arg(1, kb); node.update_arg(2, vb)
transformation.code_hint: Equivalent fast-path: torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True engages TF32 Tensor Cores without explicit casts, but BF16 yields the larger win because it also removes the fmha FP32 register spill. Guard accuracy with a tolerance check; keep LayerNorm reductions in FP32.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: GEMM operand reads halved (FP32->BF16); SDPA Q/K/V reads halved and 757760 local-memory spill wavefronts per fmha eliminated

optimizations.notes: tensor_core_active_pct = 0.0 with avg_threads_per_warp = 32.0 is an unambiguous FP32-SIMT signature; this is the single highest-leverage change. Must precede any pass that calls register_buffer (QKV fusion, pre-transpose) because fused/transposed weight buffers must be allocated at the promoted bf16 dtype. duration_ns figures are ncu-inflated (2-5x); reduction estimate is scaled to the relative time share, not absolute latency.

bottleneck.description: The three Q/K/V projections are emitted as three independent aten::mm nodes, each [4096,512] @ [512,512], each a separate 'Kernel2' launch (~74.8us). All three share the SAME left-hand activation (the post-LayerNorm tensor, input_op_ids reference the same producer), so the activation is read from DRAM three times. Each kernel launches grid [64,1,2] = 128 thread blocks; on 188 SMs that is a single wave covering only ~68% of the machine, so ~32% of SMs sit idle (wave-quantization starvation) and there are three launch/sync overheads per block.

transformation.description: Concatenate the Q, K, V weight tensors along the output dimension into one [512, 1536] weight, replace the three aten::mm nodes with a single aten::mm producing [4096, 1536], then split the result back into three [4096,512] views with aten.split (or three aten.slice) feeding the existing reshape/SDPA path. GPU-level effect: one kernel launch instead of three; the shared activation is read once instead of three times; the fused GEMM launches grid ~[192,1,2] = 384 blocks (>= 2 full waves on 188 SMs), eliminating the single-wave 32% SM-idle quantization. Combined with OPT-1 the fused GEMM runs as one large HMMA call, which is the most Tensor-Core-efficient shape.
transformation.location: FX backend pass; match the 3-sibling mm pattern that shares one activation producer and feeds the SDPA reshape chain
fx_steps: q_mm, k_mm, v_mm = <the three sibling aten.mm.default nodes sharing args[0]>
wq, wk, wv = q_mm.args[1], k_mm.args[1], v_mm.args[1]   # each [512,512]
# build fused weight buffer (bf16, after OPT-1) once at trace time:
w_fused = self.register_buffer('qkv_weight', torch.cat([wq_t, wk_t, wv_t], dim=1))  # [512,1536]
transformation.code_hint: Concatenate weights along the N (output) axis matching the mm convention act @ W. After OPT-1 the cat must happen on bf16 tensors so register_buffer allocates at the runtime dtype.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: shared [4096,512] activation read 1x instead of 3x (~24 MB/iter saved at FP32, ~12 MB at BF16); better wave occupancy raises effective SM utilization from ~68% to ~100%

optimizations.notes: Pattern detection: three aten.mm nodes whose args[0] is the same node and whose args[1] are distinct weight placeholders, all consumed by the SDPA reshape chain. Must run after OPT-1 (register_buffer for the fused weight is allocated at the promoted dtype). The latency win is modest in wall time but the launch-count and wave-occupancy improvement compounds with OPT-1's Tensor-Core engagement.

bottleneck.description: The 'Kernel2' GEMM reads its right-hand weight in a layout that forces an in-kernel transpose/repack each launch, contributing to the low sm_throughput (~36%) and the 49% L1TEX throughput with only 10.8% L1 sector hit rate (poor weight-load locality). Because weights are constant across iterations, the repack is pure redundant work. registers_per_thread = 210 also points at the GEMM epilogue/transpose path consuming the register file and capping occupancy at ~16.5%.

transformation.description: Pre-transpose (and contiguous-pack) the projection weights at compile time so the runtime GEMM consumes them in the Tensor-Core-preferred layout with no in-kernel transpose. Replace the mm weight argument with a pre-transposed constant buffer and drop any aten.t / aten.permute feeding the mm. GPU-level effect: removes the per-launch weight repack, improves L1 weight-load locality (lifts the 10.8% sector hit rate), and frees epilogue registers so occupancy can rise above the current 16.5% cap.
transformation.location: FX backend pass, after QKV fusion has rewritten the weight nodes
fx_steps: # rewrite mm to consume the pre-transposed buffer in the layout cuBLAS prefers
# if an aten.t()/aten.permute() fed args[1], erase it
if w.op == 'call_function' and w.target in (torch.ops.aten.t.default, torch.ops.aten.permute.default):
transformation.code_hint: Validate the resulting cuBLAS path actually changes (re-profile and confirm sector hit rate and occupancy rise); if cuBLAS already handles the layout internally this is a no-op. Pre-transpose buffer must be bf16 (post OPT-1).

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: eliminates per-launch weight repack; improves L1 weight reuse (10.8% -> higher sector hit rate)

optimizations.notes: Must come after OPT-2 (QKV fusion erases the original Q/K/V weight placeholder nodes; pre-transpose must act on the fused weight buffer instead). Confidence is low because the profile does not expose the cuBLAS heuristic directly and modern cuBLAS often absorbs the transpose internally on Blackwell; treat as speculative pending a re-profile. Gated behind OPT-1 (register_buffer dtype rule).

global_notes: 0 operators below 1% threshold omitted (all 13 attributed operators are >= 1% of attributed time).

device_name = 'NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition' (GB202, ~188 SMs, 5th-gen Tensor Cores). warp_cycles_per_instruction is removed on Blackwell (confirmed null throughout); bottleneck reasoning uses eligible_cycles_pct, tensor_core_active_pct, and avg_threads_per_warp instead.

edge_case_flag fused_kernel_double_count: operators op_id=14 and op_id=36 carry is_fused=true (aten::_efficient_attention_forward fused with aten::_scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention). Their fmha kernels are the same logical SDPA call; not summed twice in the per-kernel reasoning.

edge_case_flag layer_nvtx_double_count: the 'layer::unique::prologue' NVTX range (913658 ns, 41% of attributed time) is a layer-dedup wrapper that ENCLOSES the same Kernel2 (mm) and fmha kernels that torch_profiler attributes individually to aten::mm and aten::_efficient_attention_forward. Treat the prologue total as overlapping with, not additive to, the individual op totals. The bottleneck mechanism (FP32 SIMT GEMM + FP32 fmha spill) is identical regardless of which attribution layer is read.

knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were not present in the repo and no MCP tools (context7, exa-search, sequential-thinking, memory) were available. Fallback applied: FX surgery patterns drawn from embedded knowledge, novel transforms capped at medium confidence. All cited counters and the FP32-SIMT diagnosis are derived directly from profile.json and are unaffected by tool availability.
All duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu counter-collection overhead and are used only for relative comparison within this profile, never as absolute latency.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 (dtype promotion) -> OPT-2 (QKV fusion) -> OPT-3 (weight pre-transpose). Linear chain, no cycles. OPT-1 is mandatory first because every downstream pass calls register_buffer and the buffer must be allocated at the promoted bf16 dtype.

Accuracy guard: BF16 promotion changes numerics. Keep LayerNorm statistics in FP32 and validate attention output against the FP32 reference within a tolerance before shipping; if accuracy is insufficient, fall back to TF32 (torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32) which still engages Tensor Cores at higher precision.
